seo.title: 100 FastAPI resources for developers
seo.description: A curated set of practical FastAPI resources for engineers.

content.intro: A guide to building and scaling FastAPI applications with a focus on production-ready architecture, high-performance database management, and modern AI/LLM integration patterns.

items.title: Full Stack FastAPI Template
items.description: The official Tiangolo production-ready template including PostgreSQL, Docker, Celery, and a Vue frontend. Use it to understand the recommended project layout with separate services and alembic migrations.

items.title: FastAPI Best Practices Repository
items.description: A curated list of architectural patterns on GitHub (zhanymkanov/fastapi-best-practices) covering folder structure, dependency injection, and centralized exception handling.

items.title: SQLModel Integration
items.description: Use SQLModel to eliminate code duplication between Pydantic models and SQLAlchemy entities. This reduces the surface area for bugs in larger applications.

items.title: Modular APIRouter Implementation
items.description: Break down large APIs into domain-specific modules using fastapi.APIRouter. Mount them in a main.py file to maintain clean namespaces and manageable file sizes.

items.title: FastAPI Versioning
items.description: Implement the fastapi-versioning library to manage API lifecycle. It allows you to route requests based on header or URL versioning without duplicating the entire codebase.

items.title: Dependency Injection for Service Layers
items.description: Move business logic out of path operations and into service classes. Inject these services using FastAPI's Depends() to facilitate easier unit testing with mocks.

items.title: Pydantic v2 Migration
items.description: Upgrade to Pydantic v2 for up to 20x performance improvement in data validation. Ensure all Field and Validator definitions are updated to the new syntax.

items.title: FastAPI Pagination
items.description: Standardize list responses using the fastapi-pagination library. It supports multiple backends including SQLAlchemy, Tortoise, and Motor with minimal boilerplate.

items.title: Custom Exception Handlers
items.description: Register global exception handlers using @app.exception_handler to return standardized JSON error schemas across the entire API.

items.title: Cookiecutter FastAPI
items.description: A template for generating a FastAPI project with pre-configured Docker Compose, CI/CD pipelines, and environment variable management via Pydantic Settings.

items.title: SQLAlchemy 2.0 Async Session
items.description: Implement the async version of SQLAlchemy's sessionmaker. Use the async_scoped_session for thread-safe operations in high-concurrency environments.

items.title: Alembic Async Migrations
items.description: Configure Alembic to use the async driver (e.g., postgresql+asyncpg). Modify the env.py file to use run_async for database schema updates.

items.title: Redis for Response Caching
items.description: Integrate redis-py (async) to cache expensive database queries. Use the fastapi-cache2 library to implement easy decorator-based caching on path functions.

items.title: Beanie ODM for MongoDB
items.description: An asynchronous Python ODM for MongoDB based on Pydantic. It allows you to use the same models for database storage and API validation.

items.title: Tortoise ORM
items.description: A Django-inspired async ORM built specifically for FastAPI. Ideal for developers who prefer an Active Record pattern over SQLAlchemy's Data Mapper pattern.

items.title: Connection Pooling with asyncpg
items.description: Optimize PostgreSQL performance by using asyncpg's connection pool directly for high-throughput read operations that don't require a full ORM.

items.title: Database Transaction Middleware
items.description: Implement a custom middleware to handle database transactions per request, ensuring automatic rollbacks on 4xx/5xx responses.

items.title: Prisma Client Python
items.description: Use the Prisma ORM for auto-generated, type-safe database clients. It provides a superior developer experience for complex schema relationships.

items.title: Piccolo ORM
items.description: An async ORM and query builder that includes a built-in admin interface and migration system, similar to Django but optimized for asyncio.

items.title: Database Health Checks
items.description: Create a dedicated /health endpoint that pings the database and Redis to ensure the application is truly ready to receive traffic.

items.title: StreamingResponse for LLMs
items.description: Use fastapi.responses.StreamingResponse to stream tokens from OpenAI or local LLMs to the client in real-time, reducing perceived latency.

items.title: LangServe Implementation
items.description: Deploy LangChain chains as FastAPI endpoints using LangServe. It automatically generates input/output schemas and provides a playground UI.

items.title: SSEStarlette for Events
items.description: Implement Server-Sent Events (SSE) using sse-starlette to push updates from long-running AI inference tasks back to the frontend.

items.title: Ray Serve for Model Scaling
items.description: Integrate Ray Serve with FastAPI to scale compute-intensive ML models across a cluster while maintaining a standard REST interface.

items.title: FastAPI-Limiter for AI Quotas
items.description: Apply rate limiting to expensive AI endpoints using Redis-backed fastapi-limiter to prevent API abuse and manage inference costs.

items.title: Asyncio Semaphore for Backpressure
items.description: Use asyncio.Semaphore(n) to limit the number of concurrent requests to a GPU-bound model, preventing OOM (Out of Memory) errors.

items.title: BentoML for Model Serving
items.description: Package models with BentoML and serve them via FastAPI. It handles model versioning and provides optimized runners for inference.

items.title: Prometheus Monitoring for ML
items.description: Use prometheus-fastapi-instrumentator to track inference time, token usage, and model error rates for production AI services.

items.title: BackgroundTasks for Pre-processing
items.description: Offload data cleaning or vector embedding generation to FastAPI's BackgroundTasks to return immediate 202 Accepted responses to users.

items.title: vLLM Integration
items.description: Connect FastAPI to a vLLM inference server for high-throughput LLM serving, utilizing PagedAttention for efficient memory management.

items.title: FastAPI Users for Auth
items.description: Implement the fastapi-users library for a complete authentication system including registration, JWT/Cookie strategy, and password reset.

items.title: OAuth2 with Password Flow
items.description: Use FastAPI's built-in OAuth2PasswordBearer to implement secure token-based authentication without external providers.

items.title: Uvicorn Gunicorn Worker Strategy
items.description: Deploy using Gunicorn with the Uvicorn worker class (uvicorn.workers.UvicornWorker) to manage multiple processes and improve reliability.

items.title: Nginx Reverse Proxy Config
items.description: Configure Nginx with proxy_buffering off and proxy_set_header Connection 'upgrade' to support WebSockets and streaming responses.

items.title: Pydantic Settings Management
items.description: Use pydantic-settings to manage environment variables. It provides automatic validation and type casting for configuration values.

items.title: CORS Middleware Configuration
items.description: Properly configure CORSMiddleware with specific allow_origins instead of '*' to secure your API against cross-site request forgery.

items.title: Pytest with AsyncClient
items.description: Write integration tests using httpx.AsyncClient and pytest-asyncio to simulate API requests in an asynchronous test environment.

items.title: Docker Multi-stage Builds
items.description: Use multi-stage Dockerfiles to reduce image size by separating the build dependencies from the final production runtime environment.

items.title: API Key Middleware
items.description: Implement simple API Key authentication for internal service-to-service communication using a custom Security dependency.

items.title: OpenAPI Documentation Customization
items.description: Override the default Swagger UI settings in the FastAPI constructor to disable documentation in production or add custom branding.

pro_tips: Always use 'async def' for I/O bound tasks (DB, API calls) and standard 'def' for CPU bound tasks to avoid blocking the event loop.
Leverage the 'Response' parameter in path functions to set custom headers or cookies without returning a full Response object.
Use 'Annotated' types (from typing or typing_extensions) for dependencies to make your code more readable and compatible with linters.
Set 'root_path' in the FastAPI constructor when deploying behind a proxy like Nginx or Traefik to ensure Swagger UI URLs generate correctly.
Utilize 'Pydantic.Field(..., alias="camelCase")' to maintain Pythonic snake_case internally while serving camelCase to frontend consumers.
